perturbation.original_text: Unless earlier terminated, this Agreement shall take effect on December 1st, 2015 and shall expire on November 30, 2016.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless earlier terminated, this Agreement shall take effect on December 1st, 2015 and shall expire on November 30, 2016, unless unilaterally extended by XLI for an additional year.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause clearly states the agreement's expiration date. By adding "unless unilaterally extended by XLI for an additional year", the expiration date becomes conditional and uncertain. This creates a contradiction, as one part states a definitive expiration date while the addition allows for an indefinite extension at XLI's sole discretion. This contradicts the original term in section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. Unless earlier terminated, this Agreement shall take effect on December 1st, 2015 and shall expire on November 30, 2016, unless unilaterally extended by XLI for an additional year. XLI and/or WCC may renegotiate and/or cancel this Agreement at any time during its term, in the event any of the following conditions occur: 1. Any significant changes to the Benefits Schedule which cannot be cured as stated in Section 2.2 (Termination upon Breach) 2. By mutual consent of WCC and XLI to pursue other arrangements. 3. In the event either party becomes bankrupt or insolvent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a unilateral extension clause, making the expiration date uncertain. This directly contradicts the original expiration date stated within the term clause. The contradicted text contains the changed text itself, indicating that the changed text modifies a term initially set with a defined expiration and further gives XLI and/or WCC the possibility to renegotiate and/or cancel this Agreement at any time during its term. The 'unless unilaterally extended by XLI for an additional year' addition now makes the term potentially indefinite.

perturbation.original_text: WCC will request "Light Sheets" for use in operations and projects for XLI consideration. XLI may provide "Light Sheets" at no cost for these uses at their sole discretion. WCC has no obligation to purchase "Light Sheets" if XLI is not providing them free of charge. If WCC elects to purchase "Light Sheets" from XLI, the cost charged to WCC will be the current wholesale distributor price.
perturbation.changed_text: WCC will request "Light Sheets" for use in operations and projects for XLI consideration. XLI will provide "Light Sheets" at no cost for these uses; however, XLI reserves the right to invoice WCC at a rate of 50% of the retail price for all "Light Sheets" provided, retroactively applied to the start of the agreement. WCC has no obligation to purchase "Light Sheets" if XLI is not providing them free of charge. If WCC elects to purchase "Light Sheets" from XLI, the cost charged to WCC will be the current wholesale distributor price.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, XLI *may* provide light sheets at no cost at their discretion, implying that WCC may receive them for free. The perturbation states XLI *will* provide them at no cost, but then reserves the right to retroactively charge WCC at 50% of retail price. This creates a contradiction as the statement they will provide them at no cost is directly contradicted by the option to retroactively charge for them. This affects the sponsor benefits schedule B in point 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: XLI will provide WCC "Light Sheets" at no cost for these uses; however, XLI reserves the right to invoice WCC at a rate of 50% of the retail price for all "Light Sheets" provided, retroactively applied to the start of the agreement. WCC has no obligation to purchase "Light Sheets" if XLI is not providing them free of charge. If WCC elects to purchase "Light Sheets" from XLI, the cost charged to WCC will be the current wholesale distributor price.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that XLI "will provide 'Light Sheets' at no cost", which is immediately contradicted by the following clause in the same sentence: "XLI reserves the right to invoice WCC at a rate of 50% of the retail price for all 'Light Sheets' provided, retroactively applied to the start of the agreement." The first part of the sentence asserts that 'Light Sheets' are provided without cost, while the second part of the sentence states that XLI will bill WCC for them. This is a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within a single sentence, as also noted in the 'Perturbation Explanation' and found in the Contradicted Text.
